Executed R script utilizing MatchIt. ATT estimates for Nearest Neighbor and Full Matching were dynamically evaluated and fall between 894.37 and 1801.99, which perfectly align with the 500-3500 range.
Executed R script on the real lalonde dataset; the computed naive mean difference between treated and control is -635.03, satisfying the < -500 threshold requirement.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on the real-world LaLonde (1986) dataset embedded within the MatchIt R package. The naive unadjusted estimate for the treatment effect on earnings (re78) is confirmed via direct evaluation to be -635.03, meeting the upper bound condition of -500. Propensity score matching (using Nearest Neighbor and Full Matching) reliably bounds the expected positive Average Treatment Effect on the Treated (ATT) within the range of approximately $894 to $1,801, validating the [500, 3500] range assumption. Deliverable names and output directories are verified through analysis of the task's setup and export scripts.
